channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: After a Memorial Day break, Congress returns to the same problems they left behind
item.description: Congress returns to town with the same problems they left before Memorial Day: immigration funding, opposition to a new Justice Department fund, and questions about Iran war oversight.
item.content:encoded: Congress returns to town with the same problems they left before Memorial Day: immigration funding, opposition to a new Justice Department fund, and questions about Iran war oversight.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: How aid cuts are hampering the front-line response to the Ebola crisis
item.description: Aid workers in Uganda are watching an Ebola crisis unfold in neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.
item.content:encoded:
Aid workers in Uganda are watching an Ebola crisis unfold in neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.

(Image credit: Leonard Musinguzi)
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: What China's absence from a key defense summit means for regional security in Asia
item.description: At an Asian defense summit, the U.S. called on other countries to increase military spending. China didn't even send its defense minister.
item.content:encoded: At an Asian defense summit, the U.S. called on other countries to increase military spending. China didn't even send its defense minister.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: What immigrant stories in movies teach us about the American dream
item.description: On this week's Cineplexity, we explore what movies about immigrants teach us about life in America. What movies get the story right? What do they get wrong? And what stories are left untold?
item.content:encoded: On this week's Cineplexity, we explore what movies about immigrants teach us about life in America. What movies get the story right? What do they get wrong? And what stories are left untold?
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: The terrifying day the U.S.-Iran war reached a sleepy Sri Lankan town
item.description: A deadly strike during the first days of the Iran war hit far away in the Indian Ocean, jolting a quiet seaside town and showing how far the conflict's reach extends.
item.content:encoded: A deadly strike during the first days of the Iran war hit far away in the Indian Ocean, jolting a quiet seaside town and showing how far the conflict's reach extends.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: The artist Navan left Iran as a boy. He sings to "give life" to his homeland
item.description: NPR's Adrian Florido speaks with the Canadian-Iranian singer, Navan, about the fusion of French, Persian and English on his new album, Kisses on the Moon.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Florido speaks with the Canadian-Iranian singer, Navan, about the fusion of French, Persian and English on his new album, Kisses on the Moon.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: How jiu jitsu is changing life for girls and women in a Beirut refugee camp
item.description: Ongoing wars, displacement and economic instability have kept Lebanon in a state of crisis. For some girls and women in a refugee camp in Beirut, a martial arts program is bringing some stability.
item.content:encoded: Ongoing wars, displacement and economic instability have kept Lebanon in a state of crisis. For some girls and women in a refugee camp in Beirut, a martial arts program is bringing some stability.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: An entomologist ordered a mezcal, and then got curious
item.description: An entomologist researched the worms used in mescal to better understand the relationship between the creatures and specific agave plants - and the danger if harvesting increases.
item.content:encoded: An entomologist researched the worms used in mescal to better understand the relationship between the creatures and specific agave plants - and the danger if harvesting increases.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: Trump says a ceasefire extension deal with Iran is near, but core issues remain
item.description: President Trump has not yet decided whether he'll extend a ceasefire with Iran, and Israel continues to attack targets in Lebanon, in spite of a ceasefire there.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has not yet decided whether he'll extend a ceasefire with Iran, and Israel continues to attack targets in Lebanon, in spite of a ceasefire there.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Over a thousand Palestinians from Gaza held in Israeli detention without charge
item.description: A controversial law allows Israel to hold Palestinians in prison without charge or trial. Israel says it's a necessary for security, but rights groups say it leaves detainees in a legal limbo.
item.content:encoded: A controversial law allows Israel to hold Palestinians in prison without charge or trial. Israel says it's a necessary for security, but rights groups say it leaves detainees in a legal limbo.
item.dc:creator: Itay Stern
